title: Native SOTA spectral solver engine for R
product_type: R package with native numerical core

v2_cran_release.objective: Ship eigencore V2 as the first CRAN release: a certified, native-first spectral computation package for R with honest planner labels, release-grade docs, CRAN hygiene, and scoped benchmark evidence.
v2_cran_release.scope_policy: V2 is a release-hardening and publication boundary, not a broad solver-expansion milestone. Solver families or ecosystem integrations without release-grade labels, certificates, tests, and benchmark evidence are deferred to V3.
v2_cran_release.central_claim_policy: The CRAN-facing claim is scoped to promoted surfaces with current evidence: certified native-first answers with explicit planner labels, residual/backward-error certificates, no silent sparse densification, and documented reference/prototype boundaries. The unqualified fastest-path claim remains a future ambition.
release_workstreams: scope freeze and V3 deferral map
planner honesty and certificate contract audit
acceptance: A built source tarball passes R CMD check --as-cran --no-manual or has only explicitly accepted CRAN notes.
The full testthat suite passes from the current source and installed-package context where feasible.
Public API, planner labels, result fields, certificate semantics, examples, and vignettes are internally consistent.
Benchmark claims are limited to rows with time-to-certified-answer, certificate, memory, and provenance evidence.
All V2_CRAN tracker issues are closed and the mote board has no open V2_CRAN blockers.

v2_1_downstream_enablement.objective: Ship a narrowly scoped public initial-subspace seam for certified standard Hermitian continuation after the eigencore 1.0.0 CRAN line is frozen and submitted.
v2_1_downstream_enablement.release_order: The 1.0.0 CRAN candidate remains isolated on release/1.0.0 at v1.0.0-rc1. V2.1 implementation begins only after the existing CRAN-submission tracker gate closes and targets the post-CRAN development line.
v2_1_downstream_enablement.scope_policy: V2.1 may expose existing native dense/CSC Hermitian Lanczos start-block capability with a stable public contract, fresh current-operator certificates, explicit planner/result provenance, adversarial validation, and benchmark-visible operator-column accounting. It does not include reusable solver checkpoints, recurrence reuse across changed operators, cached operator-action reuse, broad matrix-free promotion, or new solver families.
v2_1_downstream_enablement.downstream_driver: The traceratio package repeatedly solves A - rho B through exported eigencore APIs and currently must label KS-ITR runs cold/restarted because no public initial-subspace argument exists.
acceptance: eig_partial() and solve.eigencore_eigen_problem() accept initial_subspace = NULL without changing cold behavior when it is absent.
At least the supported standard real Hermitian native dense and sparse CSC Lanczos paths consume a validated user-supplied starting subspace without densifying the operator.
Unsupported planner paths reject initial_subspace explicitly rather than ignoring it or relabeling a reference path as production.
Every warm solve recomputes projected quantities, residuals, orthogonality, and a fresh certificate for the current operator.

Diagnostics report start provenance, supplied/accepted/rejected/augmented columns, operator block calls, operator columns, and certification operator columns while preserving matvecs for compatibility.
Adversarial tests cover badly scaled, duplicate, rank-deficient, zero-overlap, and partially overlapping starts against independent dense oracles.
A reproducible non-quick A - rho B sequence benchmark compares cold and warm time and operator columns to a common certified answer before any performance claim is made.
The traceratio adapter can consume the exported API without eigencore::: access and retains honest cold/reference labels for unsupported matrix-free cases.

v1_ambition: Replace RSpectra for most production use cases, beat it on speed/correctness diagnostics, and establish a more mathematically expressive foundation than SciPy-style sparse linear algebra.

executive_summary.description: A ground-up spectral computation library for R focused on partial eigenvalues, eigenvectors, singular values, singular vectors, generalized eigenpairs, and structured PCA/SVD problems using native high-performance iterative algorithms.
v1_user_promises: Native block-operator core, not scalar callback loops.
Certified partial eigensolvers and SVD, with residual/backward-error reports.
True SVD algorithms, not default normal-equation SVD.
Automatic solver planning, with inspectable decisions.
RSpectra-compatible shim, so existing users can migrate.
executive_summary.opportunity: RSpectra exposes eigs() and svds() for large-scale eigenvalue/SVD problems over dense, sparse, and user-defined operator inputs and has a substantial CRAN reverse-dependency footprint, creating an opportunity across graph, genomics, text, dimensionality reduction, and sparse modeling packages.

strategic_positioning.positioning_statement: A certified, native, matrix-free spectral computation engine for R.
strategic_positioning.differentiator: The combination of mathematical expressiveness, native speed, and correctness certification.

v1_extension: Mandatory block application in the fast path.

observations: ARPACK SVD path is documented as using an eigensolver on A^*A or AA^*.
Solver-specific options are currently reserved rather than deeply exposed.
ARPACK and PROPACK docs state that singular value return order is not guaranteed.
scipy_svds.opening: Return sorted, certified, solver-diagnosed partial SVDs as the default behavior.

primme.v1_policy: Do not wrap PRIMME in V1; use it as benchmark and design inspiration.

capabilities_to_emulate: Separation of spectral transformations from solvers.
Many SVD methods including Lanczos, thick-restart Lanczos, randomized SVD, and PRIMME wrappers.
SVD residuals defined using both Av - sigma u and A^*u - sigma v.
slepc.v1_policy: Adopt this seriousness in a smaller, R-native, non-HPC form.

mathematical_first_principles.description: Model spectral computation as structured mathematical problems, not loose calls to matrix routines.

native_algorithms_not_wrappers.external_solver_plugins: Optional later; they must not define V1.

certification_by_default.requirement: Every returned eigenpair or singular triplet must be accompanied by residuals and convergence metadata.
certification_by_default.core_user_promise: We do not merely compute partial spectra. We certify them.

inspectable_automation.requirement: method = "auto" must produce a solver plan with inspectable decisions.

package_authors.description: R package authors currently depending on RSpectra, irlba, base svd(), base eigen(), or custom sparse eigensolver logic.

applied_researchers.description: Users doing PCA, spectral clustering, graph embeddings, topic models, genomics, single-cell analysis, text models, and sparse covariance methods.

numerical_power_users.description: Users who understand Krylov methods, preconditioners, shift-invert, generalized eigenproblems, and tolerances.

supported_problem_classes.current_surface: Native Hermitian paths are promoted for the scoped V1 surfaces documented in the readiness audit: structured tridiagonal sparse/diagonal defaults, scalar native dense/CSC Hermitian paths, and explicit/diagnostic block requests.
supported_problem_classes.future_scope: General sparse block-Lanczos auto promotion has a documented 2026-06-06 no-promotion decision; explicit block requests and diagnostic sparse opt-in remain available, but sparse auto() stays on the proven scalar/tridiagonal/default native paths until a redesigned candidate beats current evidence.

supported_problem_classes.current_surface: Dense and sparse CSC explicit matrices with supported targets use a native Arnoldi cycle with native refined Ritz extraction, restart diagnostics, best-attempt retention, right-residual certification, and adjoint-capable left eigenvectors with biorthogonality diagnostics. Real matrix-free callback operators keep the native callback Arnoldi cycle with native projected Ritz extraction and the same certificate/restart boundary.
supported_problem_classes.future_scope: Full Krylov-Schur or harmonic/interior nonsymmetric extraction, matrix-free refined extraction, and native complex sparse/operator inputs remain future scope.
supported_problem_classes.notes: Compatibility-grade; needed to replace RSpectra-style use

supported_problem_classes.problem: Generalized symmetric/Hermitian SPD problem A x = lambda B x
supported_problem_classes.current_surface: Sparse shifted-tridiagonal generalized SPD LOBPCG is promoted for largest/smallest targets; native matrix-free-B metrics, native shifted-diagonal/tridiagonal preconditioner diagnostics, constraints, and adversarial SPD metric rows are covered by the current generalized strict gate; dense generalized auto uses native dense LAPACK fallback; explicit generalized Lanczos has a native transformed dense/diagonal-B path including block requests inside that transformed boundary and an honest sparse-CSC reference refinement, with native tridiagonal sparse-B Thomas metric solves distinguished from general sparse-CSC reference Cholesky.
supported_problem_classes.future_scope: Arbitrary sparse-CSC metric factorization and sparse-CSC block B-orthogonal Lanczos promotion remain post-V1 work.

supported_problem_classes.current_surface: Dense standard, dense generalized SPD, diagonal standard, sparse symmetric-tridiagonal standard, and sparse/diagonal tridiagonal generalized paths are native and certify original-coordinate residuals.
supported_problem_classes.future_scope: Native general sparse LU/factorized apply and stronger user-solve ownership are explicit PRD non-goals unless a future PRD reopens them.
supported_problem_classes.notes: With explicit factorization or user solve

supported_problem_classes.problem: Rectangular partial SVD, largest singular values
supported_problem_classes.current_surface: Promoted non-quick tall/wide sparse rows pass through bounded native Gram special cases with exact two-sided certification; dense/CSC native Golub-Kahan staging and diagnostics exist.
supported_problem_classes.future_scope: General sparse and matrix-free thick-restarted SVD beyond the bounded Gram surface is not promoted in the current PRD after the 2026-06-06 hard-SVD no-promotion decision.

supported_problem_classes.current_surface: The scoped V1 release gate is green for the large exact-low-rank dense planner row. Dense double and sparse CSC QR-normalized randomized requests for largest singular values now use native randomized controllers with native sketch generation, subspace iteration, projected-core SVD, residual certification diagnostics, and q=0 early stop; sparse CSC has a green native-controller certificate/provenance contract, while sparse speed remains diagnostic rather than a promoted release claim.
supported_problem_classes.future_scope: Matrix-free randomized controllers, LU/none native controller support, broader slow-decay adaptive planner regimes, and sparse-size speed-promotion candidates are not promoted in the current PRD after the 2026-06-06 scoped native-controller/no-promotion decision.

supported_problem_classes.current_surface: Generalized sparse shifted-tridiagonal LOBPCG and broader generalized SPD contract rows are promoted; standard Hermitian LOBPCG and non-generalized preconditioner paths remain diagnostic/prototype-only after the 2026-06-06 no-promotion decision.
supported_problem_classes.future_scope: None unless a future PRD reopens standard Hermitian LOBPCG with evidence that beats the current scalar/tridiagonal eigencore default.

supported_problem_classes.current_surface: Dense matrix smallest and nearest-sigma SVD requests use exact dense fallback certificates. Sparse CSC smallest-SVD targets use a native certified Gram special case when the smaller Gram dimension is inside the explicit materialization gate, otherwise use the native certified smallest Golub-Kahan boundary without dense fallback; matrix-free smallest-SVD callbacks use a native certified smallest callback label only when explicit Frobenius norm metadata keeps scale_is_estimate = FALSE. Sparse CSC nearest/interior targets use a native full-subspace Golub-Kahan boundary without densifying the original sparse operator; matrix-free nearest/interior targets use the matching native full-subspace callback boundary only with explicit non-estimated norm metadata. Diagonal/reference prototype interior rows can certify without densification. Planner controls expose svd_target_family, svd_target_boundary, svd_target_issue, promotion_gate_issue, full_subspace_interior, materialized_gram, native_gram_eigensolver, and exact two-sided certificate policy.
supported_problem_classes.future_scope: Scalable nearest/interior sparse and matrix-free SVD support through refined Golub-Kahan, shift-invert, or another promoted non-full-subspace native route is not promoted in the current PRD after the 2026-06-06 target-boundary decision.
supported_problem_classes.notes: Smallest sparse CSC is promoted where the Gram gate or native certified Golub-Kahan boundary applies; nearest/interior now has an exact full-subspace boundary, while scalable refined/shift-invert production is not promoted in the current PRD

supported_problem_classes.current_surface: Limited to explicit shift-invert/user-solve style boundaries with honest labels and original-coordinate certification where supported.
supported_problem_classes.future_scope: Restarted matrix-free native nonsymmetric interior workflows remain V3 scope under the closed Krylov-Schur/refinement non-goal decision for the current PRD.

supported_problem_classes.current_surface: Base complex dense matrices use native dense complex LAPACK labels for Hermitian eigen, general eigen, and SVD public paths, and base complex dense operators use a native zgemm block-apply kernel with exact residual/backward-error certificates and complex orthogonality checks. The complex ABI/certificate contract remains frozen for broader promotion: R operators use dtype = 'complex', base dense operator metadata names native_operator_kernel = 'dense_complex_zgemm', native ScalarType::C128 is reserved for future sparse/matrix-free block/operator kernels, apply_adjoint means conjugate-transpose block apply, explicit dense sources use exact Frobenius scales, metadata-scale complex callbacks can be certified directly with scale_is_estimate = FALSE, and complex Gram matrices use V^* V. Complex matrix-free eigen/SVD solver operators fail with actionable future-scope messages; complex-valued Matrix/sparse inputs remain rejected rather than silently coerced. Real-valued nonsymmetric matrices may still return complex eigenpairs through eigs() for supported LR/SR/LI/SI/LM/SM target semantics.
supported_problem_classes.future_scope: Complex sparse/operator paths and broader promoted complex matrix-free benchmark rows are not promoted in the current PRD after the 2026-06-06 complex sparse/operator boundary decision; base complex dense zgemm block apply is the scoped current promotion.
supported_problem_classes.notes: Dense complex decomposition and base dense zgemm block-apply kernels are now native; complex sparse/operator promotion remains future scope.

supported_problem_classes.current_surface: Use base LAPACK for full dense decompositions and treat dense fallbacks as explicit compatibility/oracle paths.
supported_problem_classes.future_scope: None unless the PRD is revised.
supported_problem_classes.notes: Use base LAPACK for full decompositions

non_goals: automatic adapters for every R matrix ecosystem
v1_product_scope.focus_note: DelayedArray and out-of-core support can arrive later once the operator protocol is strong. V1 should focus on numerical foundations.

post_v1_tracker_map.status_policy: Promote a solver surface only when installed benchmark evidence, certificate provenance, planner labels, and no-densification tests all cover that surface. Otherwise keep the path scoped, reference-labelled, diagnostic, or future-scope.

closed_decisions.decision: Do not promote general sparse block Hermitian Lanczos auto-selection.
closed_decisions.evidence: bench-native-hermitian-gate.R --block-candidate on path_laplacian:1000, k=20 certified 20/20 but took about 0.458s time-to-certified-answer versus about 0.0446s for certified RSpectra and about 0.0073s for the current promoted eigencore path; path_laplacian:10000 did not complete within roughly two minutes in the local probe.

closed_decisions.decision: Do not promote native general sparse shift-invert LU/factorized apply or native ownership of user-supplied solve functions in the current PRD.
closed_decisions.evidence: The scoped shift-invert boundary is already explicit and green: installed 2026-06-05 strict saved evidence plus the current 2026-06-06 strict source gate pass all eight native/reference contract rows. General sparse rows use Matrix::lu_reference_factorization with sparse pivot diagnostics, cache provenance, no dense rcond, and estimated-scale certificate honesty; user-solve rows use user_supplied_solve external-cache provenance. Native labels remain reserved for eigencore_native_factorization rows.

closed_decisions.decision: Do not promote native standard Hermitian LOBPCG/preconditioner paths as a production surface in the current PRD.
closed_decisions.evidence: bench-lobpcg-preconditioned.R source evidence on path_laplacian n=200/1000/2000, k=5 certifies the native shifted-tridiagonal LOBPCG rows and beats certified external references by about 2.44x, 8.11x, and 6.44x with memory gates green, but fails the scalar-speed gate against the current eigencore default with ratios about 0.65, 0.32, and 0.14. The path remains diagnostic/prototype-labelled rather than promoted.

closed_decisions.decision: Do not promote matrix-free, LU/none, slow-decay, or broad sparse-speed randomized SVD regimes in the current PRD.
closed_decisions.evidence: The scoped native-controller boundary is complete: exact_low_rank_dense:2000x500 passes the strict release gate at about 2.55x versus certified rsvd, and low_rank_sparse:2000x500 has a green native CSC controller certificate/provenance contract. Sparse speed remains diagnostic, slow/near-low-rank baselines were not reliably certified, and matrix-free or LU/none randomized regimes remain honest reference-control boundaries unless a future PRD reopens them.

closed_decisions.decision: Do not promote arbitrary sparse-CSC metric factorization or sparse-CSC block generalized B-orthogonal Lanczos in the current PRD.
closed_decisions.evidence: Dense and diagonal SPD metrics use native transformed generalized Lanczos, including a focused block transformed row with a green generalized-lanczos-block-native contract. Sparse tridiagonal CSC B metric solves carry eigencore native Thomas provenance inside the honest reference-labelled loop; general sparse CSC B remains Matrix::Cholesky reference provenance.

closed_decisions.decision: Do not make full Krylov-Schur-style, harmonic/interior, matrix-free refined, or complex sparse/operator nonsymmetric extraction a current-PRD blocker.
closed_decisions.evidence: The scoped V1 nonsymmetric compatibility surface closed green with native dense, sparse CSC, and real matrix-free Arnoldi cycles, native projected Ritz extraction, restart diagnostics, best-attempt retention, exact right-residual certification, and adjoint-capable left-vector diagnostics. The first V2 tranche under bd-01KTF6H41S9XDN286TR3V184P4 promotes native refined Ritz extraction for dense and sparse CSC explicit matrices; full Krylov-Schur, harmonic/interior, matrix-free refined extraction, and native complex sparse/operator paths remain future scope.

closed_decisions.decision: Do not promote general sparse and matrix-free thick-restarted SVD beyond the bounded Gram/callback boundary in the current PRD.
closed_decisions.evidence: The retained native workspace and matrix-free callback-boundary slices are implemented and validated, but installed/quick hard-SVD candidate evidence remains speed or memory red versus certified RSpectra/irlba references. The path stays diagnostic, with active gate ownership moved to the umbrella for regression only.

closed_decisions.decision: Do not promote scalable refined/shift-invert sparse or matrix-free smallest/interior SVD beyond the exact certified boundaries in the current PRD.
closed_decisions.evidence: Sparse CSC smallest targets have native certified Gram/Golub-Kahan boundaries, sparse CSC interior targets have a native full-subspace Golub-Kahan boundary, exact-norm matrix-free callbacks can certify matching smallest/interior full-subspace boundaries, unsupported regimes fail loudly, and benchmark-visible target rows pass. Scalable non-full-subspace refined/shift-invert production remains V3 scope.

closed_decisions.decision: Do not promote complex sparse Matrix or complex matrix-free solver promotion in the current PRD; keep broader complex sparse/operator kernels beyond base dense zgemm future-scoped.
closed_decisions.evidence: Native dense complex LAPACK eigen/SVD paths, base dense zgemm block apply, and exact certificates are implemented; complex sparse Matrix promotion was not possible against the current Matrix surface because zMatrix is virtual and sparse complex construction is not a concrete supported path in the local Matrix version. Complex callbacks can carry exact metadata-scale certificates, while solver promotion fails with actionable future-scope messages instead of real coercion.

completed_items.completed: Complex ABI and certificate semantics are frozen before native complex promotion.
completed_items.evidence: Base complex dense operators carry dtype = 'complex', storage = 'complex_dense_matrix', native = TRUE, native_operator_kernel = 'dense_complex_zgemm', native_scalar_type = 'complex128', and conjugate-transpose adjoint metadata. Dense/operator certificate tests compare exact Frobenius-scale residuals, conjugate Gram semantics, Hermitian eigen certificates, and SVD two-sided certificates. Complex matrix-free and sparse/operator promotion remains future scope under the complex implementation child motes.

completed_items.completed: Base complex dense operators now use native zgemm block apply before complex sparse Matrix promotion.
completed_items.evidence: R/operator.R routes base complex dense apply/apply_adjoint through eigencore_dense_complex_block_apply, which calls native zgemm with conjugate-transpose adjoints and complex alpha/beta/Y support. test-operator.R and test-target-taxonomy.R require native metadata, native_operator_kernel = 'dense_complex_zgemm', exact source-scale certificates, and explicit future-scope boundaries for unsupported complex sparse/operator solver paths.

completed_items.completed: Native dense complex Hermitian eigen, general eigen, and SVD kernels are wired into public dense complex paths.
completed_items.evidence: src/small_dense.cpp registers zheev, zgeev, and zgesvd .Call wrappers; eigs_sym(), eigs(), eig_partial(), svd_partial(), and svds() now report native dense complex LAPACK labels with exact complex residual certificates. test-target-taxonomy.R, test-solvers.R, test-result-contracts.R, and test-svd-adversarial.R cover native labels, sorting, RSpectra-shaped shims, and certificate semantics; bench-nonsymmetric.R and bench-svd-surface.R include dense complex benchmark-visible rows.

completed_items.completed: Broader generalized SPD LOBPCG, preconditioner diagnostics, dense-fallback policy, constraints, and matrix-free-B promotion.
completed_items.evidence: bench-generalized-lobpcg.R --iterations=1 --strict passes the full current generalized gate: sparse generalized path smallest/largest n=1000 certifies 10/10 and passes speed/memory, dense generalized auto remains a native dense LAPACK fallback, constrained rows pass, native/reference generalized-Lanczos contracts pass, and all adversarial B rows pass with native orthogonalization labels.

completed_items.completed: nearest(sigma) planner coverage through supported shift-invert transforms.
completed_items.evidence: auto() with target = nearest(sigma) now normalizes to an implicit shift_invert(sigma) transform before planning and solve dispatch. Dense standard, sparse general reference, direct solve(eigen_problem(...)), and matrix-free-without-solve behavior are covered in test-shift-invert.R; the strict shift-invert benchmark passes all eight native/reference contract rows with original-coordinate certification and factorization provenance.

completed_items.completed: Current native operator algebra and matrix-free sidecar boundary.
completed_items.evidence: test-operator-algebra.R covers dense/CSC/diagonal scaling, sums, composition, crossprod, centering, adjoints, native provenance, and no sparse densification. bench-post-v1-operator-sidecars.R --quick --iterations=1 --strict passes matrix-free SVD, matrix-free nonsymmetric Arnoldi, and matrix-free generalized-B callback boundary rows. DelayedArray/out-of-core and automatic adapters for every R matrix ecosystem remain explicit PRD non-goals/deferred scope.

completed_items.completed: Nonsymmetric left eigenvectors and biorthogonal certificates for adjoint-capable Arnoldi paths.
completed_items.evidence: Dense, sparse CSC, and adjoint-capable real matrix-free nonsymmetric Arnoldi paths now return right_vectors, left_vectors, a separate left_residual_biorthogonal_backward_error certificate, and biorthogonality diagnostics through result accessors and the RSpectra-shaped eigs() shim. Matrix-free operators without apply_adjoint remain right-only with an explicit left-eigenvector-unavailable warning. test-result-contracts.R, test-target-taxonomy.R, and test-adversarial.R cover complex-pair real matrices, sparse/native Arnoldi rows, adjoint-capable and non-adjoint matrix-free operators, and defective/clustered nonsymmetric cases.

completed_items.completed: Native refined Ritz extraction for dense and sparse CSC nonsymmetric Arnoldi paths.
completed_items.evidence: Dense and sparse CSC nonsymmetric auto paths now report the native refined Arnoldi release-tranche label, pass arnoldi_extraction = 'refined_ritz' through planner controls and restart diagnostics, carry refined_extraction_native provenance, and explicitly mark krylov_schur = FALSE. A focused clustered/non-normal adversarial test shows native refined Ritz extraction lowering the right-residual certificate versus projected Ritz extraction; target-taxonomy, result-contract, solver, and nonsymmetric benchmark smoke tests cover the public label/certificate contract. Real matrix-free callback Arnoldi remains projected-Ritz native callback scope, and full Krylov-Schur, harmonic/interior, complex sparse/operator promotion remains future scope.

eig_partial.signature: eig_partial(A, k, target = largest(), B = NULL, method = auto(), tol = 1e-8, maxit = NULL, vectors = TRUE, seed = NULL, certify = TRUE, allow_dense_fallback = c("auto", "never", "always"), initial_subspace = NULL)

svd_partial.signature: svd_partial(A, rank, target = largest(), method = auto(), tol = 1e-8, vectors = c("both", "left", "right", "none"), seed = NULL, certify = TRUE)

examples: fit <- eig_partial(A, B = M, k = 20, target = smallest())
fit <- eig_partial(A, B = M, k = 20, target = nearest(0), method = shift_invert(sigma = 0))
fit <- svd_partial(X, rank = 100, method = randomized(oversample = 20, n_iter = 2))

initial_subspace_contract.supported_first_boundary: Standard real Hermitian native Lanczos for explicit dense double and sparse CSC operators on planner paths that explicitly declare initial-subspace support.
validation: row dimension matches the operator domain
numerical rank and accepted width satisfy the chosen method contract
orthonormalization occurs at the solver boundary
rejected or missing directions are augmented deterministically under the documented seed policy
initial_subspace_contract.certificate_semantics: The initial subspace is only a starting hint. Projected quantities, residuals, orthogonality, convergence status, and certificate are recomputed for the current operator on every solve.
initial_subspace_contract.planner_policy: Plans declare whether initial_subspace is supported. A non-NULL value on an unsupported path is an actionable error; it is never ignored, silently densified, or used to promote a reference/oracle solver label.
v2_1_non_goals: reusing Lanczos recurrences or locked convergence claims across changed operators
reusing cached operator actions across changed operators
native matrix-free, generalized, Arnoldi, LOBPCG, or SVD warm-start promotion
automatic performance or planner promotion from warm-start availability alone

problem_object_api.description: The elegant interface should be problem-first.
eigen_problem_example: P <- eigen_problem(A, metric = B, structure = hermitian(), target = nearest(0), transform = shift_invert(sigma = 0))
svd_problem_example: S <- svd_problem(A, domain = euclidean(ncol(A)), codomain = euclidean(nrow(A)), target = largest())

operator_api.requirement: Define a native block operator protocol.
operator_api.r_example: op <- linear_operator(dim = c(m, n), apply = function(X, alpha = 1, beta = 0, Y = NULL) { ... }, apply_adjoint = function(X, alpha = 1, beta = 0, Y = NULL) { ... }, dtype = "double", structure = general())
operator_api.apply_contract: returns alpha * A %*% X + beta * Y
operator_api.apply_adjoint_contract: returns alpha * A^* %*% X + beta * Y

required_v1_transforms: crossprod operator A* A, only as an operator
cyclic SVD operator, only when explicitly requested

rspectra_compatibility_shim.implementation: Route internally into the new problem API.
compatibility_goals: same broad return fields where possible
clear deprecation or upgrade warnings only when needed

current_implementation_status.implemented_path: native scalar thick-restart Hermitian Lanczos for dense double matrices and dgCMatrix operators; promoted native selected tridiagonal Hermitian LAPACK solver for symmetric tridiagonal sparse/diagonal sources with largest/smallest targets; native block Hermitian Lanczos (thick restart, locking) remains explicit/diagnostic for block requests, dense full-subspace cases, and sparse opt-in, with general sparse auto-promotion closed as a documented no-promotion decision
prototype_paths: internal reference LOBPCG spike proving preconditioner leverage on graph Laplacian smallest-eigenpair cases
explicit lobpcg() method descriptor and shifted_cholesky_preconditioner() helper routed through normal eigencore result/certificate objects with a reference-prototype planner label
native shifted tridiagonal preconditioner for path-Laplacian-style benchmark cases
typed preconditioner metadata and call-count diagnostics threaded into LOBPCG results, planner reasons, and benchmark gate rows

native standard Hermitian LOBPCG prototype for dense double and dgCMatrix operators with optional native shifted tridiagonal preconditioning; direct n = 10000, k = 20 path-Laplacian probes certify locally with the shifted tridiagonal preconditioner, but production promotion is closed as a 2026-06-06 no-promotion decision because current benchmark rows lose to the promoted scalar/tridiagonal eigencore default
native_certificate_paths: built-in dense, dgCMatrix, and diagonal standard operator certificate diagnostics
native dense generalized SPD operator certificate diagnostics
residual-backed generalized SPD operator certificate helper for future native iteration loops
native diagonal B-CholQR2 support for generalized SPD LOBPCG prototypes without dense B materialization
supported_native_targets: nearest sigma through supported shift-invert transforms
current_implementation_status.planner_policy: Use the promoted native tridiagonal label only for benchmark-proven symmetric tridiagonal sparse/diagonal regimes; keep scalar-native labels for non-tridiagonal sparse Hermitian regimes; keep sparse block labels explicit/diagnostic after the 2026-06-06 no-promotion decision; route auto() nearest(sigma) through an implicit shift_invert(sigma) transform for supported factorized regimes, and fail loudly for matrix-free/unfactorized inputs instead of remapping the target.

generalized_spd.problem: A x = lambda B x, B positive definite
generalized_spd.residual_formula: r_i = A v_i - lambda_i B v_i

v1_scope: adjoint-capable left eigenvectors with biorthogonality diagnostics
nonsymmetric_eigen_solver.v1_acceptance_boundary: Dense and sparse CSC explicit matrices must use a native Arnoldi cycle with native refined Ritz extraction, restart diagnostics, right-residual certification, and left residual/biorthogonality certification when an adjoint apply path is available. Real matrix-free callback operators remain native callback Arnoldi with native projected Ritz extraction. Unsupported user-operator and complex-valued input paths remain reference-labelled, rejected, or future-scope unless their own native ABI and gates are promoted.

partial_svd_solver.normal_equation_policy: The default must not be A^T A unless the solver planner explicitly chooses that as a safe special case.
partial_svd_solver.required_residual: r_i = (||A v_i - sigma_i u_i||^2 + ||A^* u_i - sigma_i v_i||^2)^(1/2)
required_features: orthogonality certification for U and V

current_implementation_status.implemented_path: native Golub-Kahan staging path for dense double matrices and dgCMatrix operators; real matrix-free operators with adjoints use a native callback-cycle Golub-Kahan label with native Ritz extraction; bounded native CSC left-Gram and right-Gram certified special cases cover the promoted non-quick tall/wide sparse largest-SVD release rows and sparse smallest target-contract rows with exact two-sided certification; unexported block-Golub-Kahan thick-restart oracle, native block Ritz extraction kernel, dense/CSC native block basis-cycle scaffolding, and retained IRLBA/LBD BPRO diagnostics exist for broader H
current_implementation_status.tiny_sparse_gram_gate_status: installed strict non-quick H evidence is green for the promoted eigencore tall/wide sparse surface: tall_sparse 100000 x 500 and wide_sparse 500 x 100000 certify 20/20 triplets and pass speed/memory through the bounded native Gram special case; quick 600x90 fixtures are diagnostic only and not release signoff
current_implementation_status.retained_h_candidate_status: the executable --h-candidate gate targets the promoted eigencore SVD path, with retained IRLBA/BPRO and block-GK rows left as diagnostics; installed 2026-06-05 strict non-quick tall_sparse and wide_sparse probes certify 20/20 requested triplets and pass speed/memory, with ratios 3.849574x and 9.882995x speed and 2.049985x memory versus the best certified references; source 2026-06-06 strict target contracts certify smallest_sparse:5000x500 through the native certified Gram SVD special case and interior_sparse:5000x500 through native full-subspace Golub-Kahan, with speed/memory green versus dense base target rows; retained BPRO remains diagnostic and speed/memory-red
native_certificate_paths: built-in dense, dgCMatrix, and diagonal SVD operator certificate diagnostics
not_yet_satisfied: general sparse and matrix-free thick-restarted SVD speed gates outside the narrow certified Gram production surface and callback-boundary sidecar
scalable refined/shift-invert sparse and matrix-free interior SVD beyond the full-subspace boundary
fully promoted block-native Golub-Kahan/IRLBA/BPRO performance path
thick restart / continuation without adaptive full reruns for broader sparse regimes
current_implementation_status.planner_policy: Use honest native prototype labels for dense/CSC staging paths and a native callback-boundary label for real matrix-free SVD with adjoints; broader sparse/matrix-free SVD performance promotion is closed as diagnostic-only in the current PRD unless future installed speed, memory, and exact certificate evidence reopens it. For smallest/interior SVD, expose svd_target_family, svd_target_boundary, promotion gate issue fields, closed decision issue fields, materialized_gram, and native_gram_eigensolver in planner/benchmark controls. Sparse CSC smallest-SVD may use the native certified Gram special case inside the explicit Gram gate and native certified Golub-Kahan otherwise; exact-norm matrix-free smallest-SVD callbacks may use native certified labels. Sparse CSC nearest-sigma SVD and exact-norm matrix-free nearest-sigma callbacks may use native full-subspace labels, while scalable refined/shift-invert interior SVD remains future scope.

randomized_svd.options_signature: randomized(oversample = 10, n_iter = 2, block = NULL, normalizer = c("qr", "lu", "none"), refine = TRUE)
required_behavior: warning if approximation fails requested tolerance
planner heuristics informed by HegelSVD's adaptive QB/PCS regimes without inheriting dense-only assumptions
accuracy must match or improve vendor/rsvd on singular-value and subspace-error benchmarks
time-to-certified-answer must beat vendor/rsvd in regimes where the planner selects randomized SVD
randomized_svd.reference_baseline: vendor/rsvd is the minimum public-package baseline for randomized SVD: eigencore must match or improve rsvd::rsvd() accuracy and exceed rsvd performance once certification/refinement cost is included.
randomized_svd.benchmark_surface: inst/benchmarks/bench-randomized-rsvd.R reports oracle singular-value error, left/right subspace error, true SVD certificate fields, rsvd speed ratios, whether the rsvd baseline itself passes eigencore certification, and native dense/sparse randomized controller contracts.

current_implementation_status.implemented_path: native dense randomized controller for dense double QR-normalized largest-target requests and native sparse CSC randomized controller for sparse CSC QR-normalized largest-target requests, with deterministic seed, native fused A Omega sketch generation, native QR subspace iteration, native direct Q' A projected-core construction, native projected-core SVD, native residual certification diagnostics, certified q=0 early stop, and deterministic native Gram refinement when needed; matrix-free and LU/none randomized regimes remain reference-control with native sketch/projection/projected-core kernels where available.
current_implementation_status.validated_regime: source strict 2026-06-06 exact-low-rank dense benchmark passes the scoped randomized 2x release gate versus certified rsvd at about 2.55x with randomized_controller_native = true, randomized_native_certificate_diagnostics = true, randomized_adaptive_stop_used = true, and accuracy/certificate gates green; the same strict run certifies low_rank_sparse:2000x500 through native_csc_randomized_controller with randomized_sparse_native_controller = true and the controller contract green, while sparse speed remains diagnostic; quick dense/sparse and uncertified-baseline rows remain printed diagnostics with release_gate_required = false
not_yet_satisfied: matrix-free and LU/none native randomized solver controllers beyond the dense/sparse QR native-controller slice
stable sparse-size speed-promotion candidate against certified baselines

lobpcg.required_v1_method: LOBPCG for symmetric/Hermitian standard and generalized SPD problems
lobpcg.planning_priority: Primary scalable V1 path for generalized SPD eigenproblems before B-orthogonal Lanczos.
required_features: dense, sparse, and matrix-free A/B operators
primary_v1_use_cases: largest generalized eigenpairs via sign-flipped A when appropriate

current_implementation_status.implemented_path: native generalized SPD LOBPCG for explicit/sparse/structured paths with B-orthogonal native kernels, optional constraints, explicit SPD matrix-free B contracts, and typed shifted-diagonal / shifted-tridiagonal preconditioners; dense generalized auto uses the native dense generalized SPD LAPACK fallback.
current_implementation_status.validated_regime: current 2026-06-06 one-iteration strict generalized benchmark certifies sparse_generalized_path_smallest:1000 and sparse_generalized_path_largest:1000 at 10/10 with shifted-tridiagonal preconditioner provenance, native B-orthogonal execution, no sparse densification, and speed/memory gates green versus dense base; the same full gate passes dense fallback policy, constraints, adversarial diagonal/sparse-CSC/matrix-free B rows, generalized-Lanczos native/reference contracts, and sparse CSC metric-boundary rows that distinguish native tridiagonal Thomas solves from general sparse reference Cholesky
not_yet_satisfied: arbitrary sparse-CSC metric factorization and sparse-CSC block generalized B-orthogonal Lanczos beyond the dense/diagonal transformed native block slice and tridiagonal sparse-B native Thomas metric-solve boundary

shift_invert.scope: Cover the core normal shift-invert case and expose user-supplied solve operators cleanly.
required_features: residuals computed in original problem, not transformed problem only

current_implementation_status.implemented_path: native factorized Lanczos shift-invert for dense standard Hermitian, dense generalized SPD, diagonal standard, sparse symmetric-tridiagonal standard, and sparse/diagonal tridiagonal generalized SPD cases; honest reference-labelled sparse-LU and user-solve boundaries remain for general sparse and matrix-free solves. All paths return eigenvalues in original coordinates with operator-side certification and attach shift_invert_factorization_contract_v1 ownership metadata.
honest_planner_labels: native dense generalized SPD shift-invert (factorized Lanczos)
native tridiagonal generalized SPD shift-invert (factorized Lanczos)
reference generalized SPD Lanczos shift-invert (sparse LU)

factorization_ownership_model.native_contract: Native labels require eigencore-owned factorized apply, cache-key provenance, no dense fallback, and original-coordinate certification.
factorization_ownership_model.matrix_reference_contract: General sparse rows use Matrix::lu_reference_factorization with sparse pivot diagnostics, cache invalidation by A/B/sigma/structure fingerprint, no dense rcond, and estimated-scale certificate honesty.
factorization_ownership_model.user_solve_contract: User-supplied solve functions are user_supplied_solve external-cache boundaries with original-coordinate certification and no native ownership claim.

guards: near-singular shifted operator detected via dense LU or native tridiagonal pivot diagnostics and rejected with an actionable error
general sparse reference rows expose sparse LU pivot-ratio diagnostics without dense rcond
zero-magnitude inverted eigenvalue detected and rejected with an actionable error
strict benchmark contracts require shift_invert_factorization_contract_v1 metadata and forbid native labels unless the contract provider is eigencore_native_factorization
unsupported sparse or matrix-free generalized metrics reject instead of densifying silently
closed_non_goals: native general sparse LU/factorized apply promotion beyond the current Matrix::lu reference factorization contract
native ownership of user-supplied solve functions beyond the current external-cache contract
current_implementation_status.validated_regime: installed 2026-06-05 strict saved shift-invert benchmark and current 2026-06-06 strict source gate pass all eight native/reference contract rows: dense standard, dense generalized, sparse tridiagonal, diagonal standard, sparse tridiagonal generalized, sparse general reference, sparse/diagonal generalized reference, and matrix-free user-solve reference
current_implementation_status.planner_policy: Use native labels only for dense and diagonal/tridiagonal factorized Lanczos regimes whose factorization contract provider is eigencore_native_factorization. General sparse and user-solve shift-invert remain explicitly reference-labelled with cache/factorization provenance, no silent sparse densification, and contract-gated ownership metadata.

solver_planner.requirement: V1 must include an inspectable planner.

v1_rules.default_method: LOBPCG first; B-orthogonal block Lanczos as alternate/refinement

core_architecture.suggested_core_language: C++17 or C++20 with a stable C ABI boundary for R.
memory_requirements: explicit allocation counters in debug mode
no R allocations inside native iteration for built-in operators

operator_performance_contract.r_callback_policy: R callback operators are allowed but marked as slower. The planner should warn if a user gives only scalar matvec and the selected method would benefit from block application.

required_methods: Cholesky QR2 for appropriate block bases
acceptance_metrics: max_abs(Q^* B Q - I) <= orthogonality_tol for generalized problems
orthogonalization_requirements.default_tolerance_policy: Default orthogonality_tol should be tied to machine precision and problem conditioning, not hard-coded naively.

returns.certificate_type: character provenance tag, currently residual_backward_error or uncomputed
returns.norm_bound_type: character norm-source tag such as frobenius_exact, frobenius_metadata, identity_exact, or frobenius_hutchinson_estimate

functional: eig_partial() works for dense real, sparse real, symmetric sparse, and matrix-free operators.
svd_partial() works for dense real, sparse real, and matrix-free operators with adjoints.
eig_partial(A, B = B) supports generalized symmetric/Hermitian SPD problems.
shift_invert(sigma) works for explicit sparse/dense symmetric problems and user-supplied solve operators.
svd_partial() returns singular values sorted in requested order.
All high-level results include nconv, residuals, method name, iteration count, operator-call counts, and certificate status.
RSpectra-compatible eigs(), eigs_sym(), and svds() shims exist.
All built-in explicit matrix paths run without R callbacks inside the iteration loop.
The package passes CRAN-style checks, sanitizer checks, and valgrind-style memory checks on representative platforms.

small_matrix_ground_truth_requirements: eigenvalue relative error <= 100 * eps-scaled bound when well-conditioned
singular value relative error <= 100 * eps-scaled bound when well-conditioned
subspace angle small for clustered/multiple values
residual certificate agrees with direct recomputation
all_tested_matrix_requirements: reported residuals must match independently recomputed residuals
reported orthogonality must match independently recomputed orthogonality
certified converged results must satisfy requested tolerance
non-converged results must be clearly marked

acceptance: max combined residual <= requested tolerance scale
U^T U close to I for returned U
V^T V close to I for returned V
clustered singular values judged by subspace accuracy, not individual vector identity

spd_b_requirements: A V approximately B V Lambda
invalid_b_handling: not positive definite when factorization proves failure

v1_release_gates: Hermitian sparse eigen: median time-to-certified-answer at least 1.25x faster than RSpectra on the V1 benchmark suite, with no worse residual quality.
Partial SVD largest singular values: median time-to-certified-answer at least 1.1x faster than RSpectra on benchmark cases where normal-equation SVD is disadvantaged.
Randomized SVD: at least 2x faster than deterministic Lanczos SVD on low-rank-ish matrices when tolerance is loose, while clearly reporting approximation quality.
Memory: no materialization of A^T A or A A^T in default SVD paths.
Allocation: no per-iteration dynamic allocation in native hot loops after setup for built-in matrix operators.
R boundary: for built-in matrix types, zero R-level callbacks during solver iteration.
Fallback honesty: if a benchmark is slower than RSpectra, the certificate must show whether the new solver achieved stricter correctness; otherwise the case is a performance failure.

api.usability_goal: No user should need to understand Arnoldi, Lanczos, or Golub-Kahan to get a reliable result.

operator_adjoint_tests.requirement: Required for SVD unless user explicitly opts out of vector computation.

validation_plan.adversarial_acceptance_basis: Residuals and subspace angles, not naive vector-by-vector comparison.

compare_against: SciPy as an external reference benchmark where useful
PRIMME as an external reference benchmark where useful

pr_requirements_for_solver_logic: randomized property tests with fixed seeds
continuous_validation.release_requirement: Full benchmark suite runs before release.

v1_release_milestones.name: Milestone 0
v1_release_milestones.title: mathematical spec
exit_criteria: all V1 problem classes mapped to solver candidates

v1_release_milestones.name: Milestone 1
v1_release_milestones.title: native operator core
exit_criteria: no R callback for built-in dense/sparse operators

v1_release_milestones.name: Milestone 3
v1_release_milestones.title: Hermitian eigensolver
exit_criteria: beats or matches RSpectra on initial Hermitian benchmark subset
v1_release_milestones.current_status: G1 green for the promoted structured-tridiagonal Hermitian default: certified native scalar thick-restart remains available for dense/CSC Hermitian problems, and symmetric tridiagonal sparse/diagonal sources with largest/smallest targets now dispatch through a native selected tridiagonal LAPACK solver with a tridiagonal residual certificate. Installed strict path_laplacian:1000 evidence from 2026-06-05 certifies 20/20 and passes speed, memory, and PRIMME parity. Native block Hermitian Lanczos (thick restart, locking) remains available for explicit requests, dense full-subspace cases, and diagnostic sparse opt-in; general sparse block auto-promotion is closed by a 2026-06-06 documented no-promotion decision after current non-quick evidence certified but failed speed/parity. Native standard Hermitian LOBPCG with shifted tridiagonal preconditioning remains diagnostic/prototype-only after a 2026-06-06 no-promotion decision: it certifies and beats external references but fails the scalar-speed gate against the current eigencore default. The generalized SPD LOBPCG strict gate is green for shifted-tridiagonal, matrix-free-B, constrained, adversarial-B, generalized-Lanczos native/reference/block contract rows, and sparse CSC metric-boundary diagnostics; arbitrary sparse-CSC metric factorization and sparse-CSC block generalized Lanczos are not claimed.

v1_release_milestones.name: Milestone 4
v1_release_milestones.title: generalized SPD LOBPCG
deliverables: largest and smallest generalized eigenpair targets
exit_criteria: A V approximately B V Lambda
sparse A/B inputs are not densified
largest and smallest generalized targets pass adversarial B cases
v1_release_milestones.current_status: Scoped V1 gate green for the promoted sparse shifted-tridiagonal generalized LOBPCG surface. Current 2026-06-06 one-iteration strict evidence certifies largest and smallest sparse generalized targets at n = 1000, k = 10 with native B-orthogonal execution, shifted-tridiagonal preconditioner provenance, and no sparse densification; the same full gate also passes dense generalized fallback policy rows, native matrix-free-B metrics, native constraints, adversarial diagonal/sparse-CSC/matrix-free B rows, and generalized-Lanczos native/reference contracts. Dense generalized auto remains a native dense LAPACK fallback rather than an iterative promotion.

v1_release_milestones.name: Milestone 4b
v1_release_milestones.title: generalized SPD Lanczos and shift-invert
exit_criteria: B-orthogonal Lanczos agrees with LOBPCG certificates on shared cases
sigma-targeted problems converge on validation cases
v1_release_milestones.current_status: Scoped V1 K gate now has a native transformed generalized SPD B-orthogonal Lanczos slice for dense and diagonal SPD metrics, including explicit block requests inside that transformed boundary, with original-coordinate generalized residual certification and B-orthogonality. Sparse CSC SPD metrics remain honestly reference-labelled at the solver-loop level, but tridiagonal sparse-B metrics use eigencore-owned native Thomas metric solves while general sparse CSC metrics retain Matrix::Cholesky reference provenance. The focused strict block row certifies diagonal_generalized_block_lanczos_native_smallest with block_size = 2 and a green block-native contract. These boundaries preserve B-orthogonality, avoid sparse densification, and agree with native generalized LOBPCG certificates. Arbitrary sparse-CSC metric factorization and sparse-CSC block generalized Lanczos promotion are not claimed.

v1_release_milestones.name: Milestone 5
v1_release_milestones.title: SVD solver
exit_criteria: beats RSpectra on SVD benchmark subset
v1_release_milestones.current_status: Scoped V1 H gate is green for the promoted non-quick tall/wide sparse surface through bounded native Gram special cases with exact two-sided certificates and installed 2026-06-05 evidence, and source 2026-06-06 strict target contracts are green for sparse smallest/interior rows. Smallest sparse CSC uses the native certified Gram SVD special case inside the explicit Gram-dimension gate; interior sparse CSC uses native full-subspace Golub-Kahan. Dense/CSC native Golub-Kahan staging, block-Golub-Kahan oracle machinery, retained IRLBA/LBD diagnostics, and BPRO diagnostics remain benchmark-visible but are not a general sparse or matrix-free thick-restart production SVD claim. Scalable refined/shift-invert interior SVD is not promoted in the current PRD after the closed 2026-06-06 target-boundary decision under bd-01KTEH6862GB19JJWX2M3FQP6T.

v1_release_milestones.name: Milestone 6
v1_release_milestones.title: randomized SVD
exit_criteria: fast approximate results on low-rank-ish matrices
accuracy matches or improves vendor/rsvd on the randomized benchmark bank
time-to-certified-answer beats vendor/rsvd in randomized-planner regimes
planner chooses randomized PCS only in regimes where certification and benchmarks justify it
v1_release_milestones.current_status: Scoped V1 randomized gate is green for the large exact-low-rank dense planner row. Dense double and sparse CSC QR-normalized largest-target requests now use native randomized controllers with exact residual certification diagnostics and q=0 early stop; sparse CSC has a green native-controller certificate/provenance contract but sparse speed remains diagnostic. Quick small-size, slow-decay, uncertified-baseline, matrix-free, and LU/none rows are diagnostic or future scope after the closed 2026-06-06 native-controller/no-promotion decision under bd-01KTE8JFKPA90ZJTXK496SBMK4.

v1_release_milestones.name: Milestone 7
v1_release_milestones.title: LOBPCG
v1_release_milestones.current_status: Generalized LOBPCG is green for sparse shifted-tridiagonal largest/smallest targets and the broader generalized strict contract bank with current 2026-06-06 evidence: B-orthogonal native execution, shifted native preconditioner provenance, matrix-free-B metrics, constraints, adversarial B rows, dense fallback policy, generalized-Lanczos native/reference/block contracts, sparse CSC metric-boundary diagnostics, and no sparse densification. Standard Hermitian LOBPCG/preconditioner promotion is closed as diagnostic/prototype-only under bd-01KTEH4G1QPR4RT14B4G78PF1M after certified rows failed the scalar-speed gate; sparse-CSC block generalized Lanczos and arbitrary sparse-CSC metric factorization are not claimed.

v1_release_milestones.name: Milestone 8
v1_release_milestones.title: R API and compatibility
v1_release_milestones.current_status: Scoped V1 public API, result objects, target taxonomy, RSpectra-compatible shims, planner labels, and documentation examples are covered by the current tests and V1 documentation audit. This is not an unlimited compatibility promise: base complex dense matrices now use native dense complex LAPACK labels with exact eigen/SVD certificates and native zgemm block apply, while complex sparse Matrix inputs and promoted complex matrix-free operator paths remain future scope; real matrices may still return complex eigenpairs through the supported general-eigen target taxonomy; broader matrix-free boundaries and unstable helper exports remain non-public.

v1_release_milestones.name: Milestone 9
v1_release_milestones.title: release hardening
v1_release_milestones.current_status: Scoped V2 CRAN release hardening requires fresh evidence after the release-version metadata update: full test suite, tarball CRAN-like check, strict installed benchmark reports for promoted surfaces, UBSan installed native smoke where available, migration docs, known limitations, and release audits. The current post-V1 tracker map has no open implementation owner rows after the 2026-06-06 no-promotion/non-goal decisions; benchmark gates remain umbrella-owned regression evidence, and future claims require a revised PRD plus installed strict or long evidence updates.

v2_scope.objective: Prepare the scoped native-first spectral engine for CRAN submission.
v2_scope.scope_policy: No new solver family is required for V2. V2 freezes scope, stabilizes the public API, audits planner labels and certificates, hardens tests, curates benchmark evidence, and makes examples, README, vignettes, package metadata, and build/check hygiene CRAN-safe.
v2_scope.acceptance_theme: V2 is ready only when the package can be built and checked as a CRAN release candidate, with every public claim tied to current certificate/provenance/test/benchmark evidence.

v2_1_scope.objective: Unblock certified basis-only spectral continuation for traceratio and similar downstream packages without reopening the eigencore 1.0.0 CRAN candidate or importing hard V3 solver expansion.
v2_1_scope.release_boundary: Development occurs from main at version 1.0.0.9000 after release/1.0.0 and v1.0.0-rc1 are frozen at commit 62dffdf. The implementation tracker items remain blocked until the 1.0.0 CRAN-submission gate closes.
release_surfaces: standard real Hermitian native dense and sparse CSC Lanczos
warm-start provenance and minimal operator-column work accounting
v2_1_scope.acceptance_theme: A user-supplied basis may accelerate a supported solve but never weakens planner honesty, current-operator certification, sparse memory policy, or benchmark discipline.

algorithmic_additions: reusable restart spaces and broad matrix-free/generalized continuation
product_additions: pca_problem(A, center = TRUE, scale = TRUE, row_weights = NULL, col_weights = NULL)
broad_matrix_ecosystem_adapters: automatic adapters for broad matrix ecosystems
v3_scope.policy: V3 owns hard solver expansion and ecosystem breadth after the V2 CRAN release. It must not compromise the V1/V2 native core; external backends should plug into the problem/solver/certificate system, not replace it.

key_vignettes: Certified partial SVD for sparse matrices

required_truths: The default SVD is not normal-equation SVD.
Generalized SPD eigenproblems are built in.
Benchmarks measure time to certified answer.
The native core avoids R-level iteration overhead.
disruptive_design_choices.central_product_claim: The fastest path to a trustworthy partial spectrum in R.
disruptive_design_choices.claim_status: Post-V2 ambition, not an unqualified CRAN-facing claim across all problem classes.
disruptive_design_choices.current_scoped_claim: For promoted V2 CRAN release surfaces, eigencore is a certified native-first spectral engine with explicit planner labels, residual/backward-error certificates, and documented reference/prototype boundaries.
disruptive_design_choices.clarification: Not merely the fastest path to some eigenvalues. A trustworthy, certified result. The unqualified fastest-path claim is valid only for surfaces with current installed benchmark evidence; all other surfaces must stay scoped, diagnostic, reference-labelled, or future-scope until their tracker gate closes.
